items.text:
DORMITORY PARKING POLICY UPDATE — Effective April 15

The Office of Residence Life is issuing the following changes to the resident parking program at Beckett Hall and Carlton Hall:

- All overnight parking permits will move from monthly billing to semester billing. The new charge is $85 per semester, due at the start of each term. Payments may be made online through the housing portal or in person at the Residence Life office in Carlton Hall, Room 110.
- Visitor passes will be available for purchase at the front desk between 7:00 AM and 9:00 PM at a rate of $3 per night. A maximum of three visitor nights per resident is permitted each month.
- Residents must register their vehicles in the housing portal by April 30. Unregistered vehicles will be ticketed beginning May 1, and any ticket exceeding $50 will require an appeal hearing.

Questions regarding billing or vehicle registration should be directed to the Residence Life office at extension 4422.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how often will residents now be charged for overnight parking?

options.A: Once per academic year for all residents
options.B: Once per semester at the start of each term
options.C: Once per month for each registered vehicle
options.D: Once per week through the housing portal

questions.explanation: The notice states overnight parking permits will move from monthly to semester billing, with the charge due at the start of each term.

questions.stem: What is the maximum number of visitor nights each resident may use per month?
questions.explanation: The notice states a maximum of three visitor nights per resident is permitted each month.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about residents who fail to register their vehicles by April 30?

options.A: They will lose their parking privileges for the entire year
options.B: They may receive tickets that could lead to an appeal hearing
options.C: They will be charged a higher semester rate automatically
options.D: They will need to switch to a different residence hall

questions.explanation: The notice says unregistered vehicles will be ticketed beginning May 1, and tickets exceeding $50 require an appeal hearing.
